# UC-17.3.61 · SonicWall Cloud SWG and SMA Access Events (SonicWall)

## Description

SonicWall Cloud SWG and Secure Mobile Access (SMA) provide cloud-delivered web security and ZTNA for remote and branch users. Tracking blocked connections, authentication events, and policy actions validates secure access enforcement.

## Value

SonicWall Cloud SWG and Secure Mobile Access (SMA) provide cloud-delivered web security and ZTNA for remote and branch users. Tracking blocked connections, authentication events, and policy actions validates secure access enforcement.

## Implementation

Forward SonicWall Cloud SWG/SMA logs via syslog or use the SMA 1000 TA. Map `cat` (web category) to acceptable-use policy. Alert on authentication failures from SMA. Report on policy enforcement effectiveness.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SonicWall SMA 1000 TA (Splunkbase 6670), `dell:sonicwall:firewall` syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=dell:sonicwall:firewall` (syslog), SMA 1000 logs via TA.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward SonicWall Cloud SWG/SMA logs via syslog or use the SMA 1000 TA. Map `cat` (web category) to acceptable-use policy. Alert on authentication failures from SMA. Report on policy enforcement effectiveness.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall" earliest=-7d
| where fw_action IN ("block","deny","drop")
| stats count by cat, usr, fw_action, rule
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**SonicWall Cloud SWG and SMA Access Events (SonicWall)** — SonicWall Cloud SWG and Secure Mobile Access (SMA) provide cloud-delivered web security and ZTNA for remote and branch users. Tracking blocked connections, authentication events, and policy actions validates secure access enforcement.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=dell:sonicwall:firewall` (syslog), SMA 1000 logs via TA. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SonicWall SMA 1000 TA (Splunkbase 6670), `dell:sonicwall:firewall` syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: proxy; **sourcetype**: dell:sonicwall:firewall. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=proxy, sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where fw_action IN ("block","deny","drop")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cat, usr, fw_action, rule** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the vendor admin console that produced the feed (see Data sources and App) for the same entities and time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top blocked users), Line chart (daily block trend), Pie chart (action distribution).

## SPL

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall" earliest=-7d
| where fw_action IN ("block","deny","drop")
| stats count by cat, usr, fw_action, rule
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top blocked users), Line chart (daily block trend), Pie chart (action distribution).
